steps.title: Identify the y-intercept of the line of best fit for data set A
steps.content: Trace the line of best fit on the scatterplot. Extend it to where it crosses the y-axis (at $x = 0$). The line intersects the y-axis at approximately $y = 52.75$ for data set A.

steps.title: Apply the transformation to the line
steps.content:
Data set B is created by subtracting 11 from every $y$-value in data set A. When you shift all data points down by the same amount, the line of best fit shifts down by that same amount. Therefore, the new y-intercept is:

$$b_{B} = b_{A} - 11 = 52.75 - 11 = 41.75$$

The value 41.75 is closest to 42.75. That matches choice C — C is correct.
